# SRC026 - How B2B SaaS Marketing Leaders Buy Software in 2024

## Metadata

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Section | B2B SaaS Buying |
| Authors / source | Wynter |
| Year | 2024 |
| Source type | Vendor buyer survey |
| Quality | Medium |
| URL | [https://info.wynter.com/hubfs/B2B%20buying%20journey%20study%20by%20Wynter.pdf](https://info.wynter.com/hubfs/B2B%20buying%20journey%20study%20by%20Wynter.pdf) |

## Bias / limitation

Messaging-testing vendor; sample limited to marketing leaders.

## Core takeaway

Shortlists are built through Google, review sites, and peers before sales calls.

## How the marketing agent should use it

Use to win the invisible shortlist: search pages, comparison pages, category pages, peer-proof content.

## Tactics unlocked

- Convert source insight into a campaign hypothesis.
- Translate the hypothesis into one ad angle, one landing-page proof section, and one measurement plan.
- Record whether the tactic worked in `memory/tactic_performance.md`.

## Agent instruction

When this source is relevant, write one sentence explaining **why this source applies to the buyer state** before using it.

## Research extraction template

```markdown
### Source extraction

- Source ID: SRC026
- What it changes about our strategy:
- What it proves:
- What it does not prove:
- Ad tactic suggested:
- Landing page proof suggested:
- Measurement implication:
- Follow-up source needed:
```
